title: Icon Wrapper
description: A container for icons that standardizes sizing (16/20/24/32/48px) and applies the current text color.
whenToUse: When placing standalone Material Symbols icons that need sizing standardization. To apply a specific icon color via CSS variables.
whenNotToUse: For icons inside components that already handle sizing (e.g. buttons). Do not use for decorative shapes.
acceptanceCriteria: Renders at all six documented sizes (16/20/24/32/48/64)
Inherits color from currentColor unless --_iw-color is overridden
When used decoratively, the inner icon has aria-hidden="true"

props.name: data-size
props.description: Icon wrapper size in pixels. Maps to internal --_iw-size token.

slots.name: default
slots.description: A single Material Symbols Outlined icon. The wrapper sets the icon's font-size and inherits color from the surrounding text.

states.name: default
states.description: Static icon container; no interactive states

states.name: not-applicable
states.description: The wrapper is not focusable or interactive — interactivity belongs to the parent element

contentGuidelines: Icons should reinforce, not replace, a text label. Reserve standalone icons for highly recognizable affordances (close, back, search).

dos: Add aria-hidden="true" on the inner Material Symbols span when the icon is decorative
Pick a size from the documented enum — don't introduce one-off sizes
donts: Don't use icon-wrapper for shapes or illustrations — only for Material Symbols icons
Don't put icon-wrapper inside a button when the host component already handles icon sizing (e.g. Button, Dropdown)

keyboard.action: Not focusable; place icon-wrapper inside an interactive element if needed

screenReader.announcement: Icon should carry aria-hidden="true" so screen readers skip it; meaning lives in the visible label
Author must add an aria-label or visually-hidden text to the parent element

wcag.howMet: Decorative icons are aria-hidden; meaningful icons require a text alternative on their host

_legacyAccessibilityNotes: If the icon conveys meaning, wrap or label it accessibly (aria-label). Decorative icons should have aria-hidden="true".
